Operation 'gettraceslist' has semantic quality issues: missing_input_details, missing_output_details. data.current_description: Get a list of vibration traces for a given time range.
data.description_suggestion: Retrieve a list of available vibration traces for a specified measuring point within a given time range. Returns trace metadata including identifiers and timestamps, enabling selection of specific traces for detailed analysis.
data.reasoning: Current description lacks clarity about required inputs (measuring point is not explicitly mentioned) and output details (what metadata is returned). Enhanced description specifies the measuring point parameter, clarifies that metadata is returned, and explains the operation's role in the workflow (enabling trace selection). This improves AI agent interpretability for determining when and how to use this operation.

data.current_description: Authenticate user and return a temporary API token.
data.summary_suggestion: Authenticate user and obtain API token
data.description_suggestion: Authenticates a user with provided credentials and returns a temporary API token for subsequent API requests. The token can be used as a query parameter in authenticated endpoints. Tokens are temporary and may expire based on system policies.
data.reasoning: Original description lacks clarity on input requirements (credentials), token usage mechanism, and token lifecycle. Enhanced version specifies credential-based authentication, token usage as query parameter (consistent with security scheme), and token expiration context for better AI agent understanding.

data.current_description: Fetch peak particle velocity and acceleration records for a given measuring point within a time range.
data.summary_suggestion: Retrieve peak vibration records for measuring point
data.description_suggestion: Retrieves peak particle velocity and acceleration vibration records for a specified measuring point within a defined time range. Requires measuring point identifier, start time, end time, and optional filtering parameters. Returns an array of peak records with timestamps, velocity values, and acceleration values. Records are ordered chronologically.
data.reasoning: Original description lacks clarity on required inputs (measuring point ID, time range specifics) and output structure. Enhanced version specifies all required parameters, clarifies the time range requirement, describes the return format and ordering, and uses domain-appropriate terminology (peak particle velocity, acceleration) for better AI agent understanding of data retrieval scope.
